meta.organizer: ZEAL COLLEGE OF ENGINEERING & RESEARCH, PUNE

rules: There will be 14 total franchises.
Each franchise must consist of 1 to 5 members acting as team management.
One member must be designated as the Lead Bidder, who is the only person permitted to place bids.
Internal discussion is allowed, but members are not allowed to interrupt other bidders or the auctioneer.
Each franchise must include exactly 12 players.
Two Wicketkeepers (WK) are compulsory; failure to include them or to complete the 12-player squad will result in disqualification.
There is no restriction on the number of overseas players.
Each franchise begins with a game purse of ₹100 crore (not a real amount).
Budgets cannot be exceeded under any circumstances.
If a team overspends or fails to complete its 12-player squad, it will be disqualified.
Loans, trades, or refunds are strictly prohibited.
The total player pool includes 170 players.
To save time, the management will allot three players (having the same points) to each team at the start.
Each player has a base price set by the auctioneer.
The auction is a single-round event, meaning sold players are removed and unsold players are permanently eliminated.
Bidders can freely call out any higher amount, as there is no fixed increment step.
The auctioneer announces the player's name and base price, and confirms the final price with "SOLD!".
Player Ratings: Each player is given a pre-assigned rating out of 10, decided by the auctioneer based on their past IPL performance (2023, 2024, 2025). These ratings are final.
Team Scoring: The team's total score is the sum of the ratings of its 12 players. The team with the highest combined rating wins.
Verification: Checks performed after the auction confirm the team has 12 players, meets the 2WK requirement, has not exceeded the ₹100 crore budget, and that player ratings are correctly recorded.
If teams have the same total rating, the team with the higher remaining budget will be declared the winner.
If still tied, the team with more players rated 9.0 or above will win.
If the tie persists, the winner will be determined by a coin toss by the auctioneer.
Immediate disqualification occurs if a franchise exceeds the ₹100 crore budget, fails to complete a 12-player team, or causes repeated disruption despite prior warnings.
The Target Lock Card can be used only 2 times.
When used for a player, the team gets one opportunity to bid, which must be their maximum offer.
After placing this bid, the team cannot participate in further bidding for that player.
If another team places a higher bid, the player is awarded to that team, and they receive double points for that particular player.

name: Code Relay Race

meta.organizer: ZEAL COLLEGE OF ENGINEERING & RESEARCH, PUNE

objective: To enhance teamwork, coding skills, and logical thinking by collaboratively completing a programming task.
description: Code Relay Race is a team-based technical game where participants solve a coding problem in a relay format. Each team member contributes by writing a part of the code within a limited time. The next member continues the code from where the previous one stopped. The aim is to complete the program correctly within the given time.
outcome: Participants will improve their coding ability, teamwork, and problem-solving skills in a fun and competitive way.
conclusion: Code Relay Race is an engaging and interactive game that promotes collaboration and enhances programming skills among participants.

description: Vibe Coding is a unique coding event where participants solve problems or build small projects in a fun, creative, and stress-free environment. Instead of strict competitive coding, participants code with music, themes, or random challenges to maintain a “vibe”. The focus is on creativity, innovation, and enjoying the coding process.
rules: Participants can participate individually (solo participation only).
Each participant can register only once.
The registration fee is non-refundable.
Multidisciplinary participation is encouraged.
Participants must be present in the allocated area during the event.
All work must be created during the competition.
Participants must present their solution in the form of a working project or prototype.
Projects must align with the given competition themes, prompts, or challenges.
Participants can work on any solution within the given problem statements, following event guidelines.
The competition will be of 4 hours, and the time limit must be strictly followed.
The use of pre-existing code or projects is not allowed, except for open-source libraries, frameworks, or AI tools permitted by the organizers.
All code and creative output developed during the competition must be original work of the participant.
Participants cannot use code, assets, or generated content from others without proper permissions or licenses.
Participants may seek help from mentors or organizers, but collaboration with other participants is not allowed.
Participants must submit their project before the designated deadline.
Submission must include project description, source code, presentation, and Git repository link (submission of Git repository is mandatory).
Late submissions may result in penalties or disqualification.
Organizers may conduct validation of code and outputs to ensure compliance with rules.
Each participant must present their project to the judging panel.
The presentation should demonstrate functionality, creativity, user experience, and overall vibe/innovation.
Judging criteria will be communicated before the event and may include creativity,

name: Bridge Nova

meta.organizer: ZEAL COLLEGE OF ENGINEERING & RESEARCH, PUNE

description: Bridge Nova is a technical and creative event where participants design and construct bridge models using given materials. The objective is to test structural strength, design efficiency, and innovation. Participants must ensure that their bridge can withstand maximum load while maintaining stability and optimal use of materials.
rules: Participants must form teams of 2 to 4 members.
Each participant can be part of only one team.
The registration fee is non-refundable.
All team members must be present during the event.
Participants must construct the bridge within the given time.
Only provided materials should be used for construction.
Pre-built models are strictly not allowed.
Bridge dimensions must follow specified constraints.
The bridge must be self-supporting.
Any damage or rule violation may lead to disqualification.
Participants must follow safety guidelines during construction.
procedure: Teams are briefed about the rules and materials.
Design planning phase is conducted.
Construction of the bridge begins.
Final bridge models are evaluated.
Load testing is performed to determine strength.
Results are declared based on performance.
outcome: Participants gain practical knowledge of structural design, teamwork, and problem-solving while applying engineering concepts in real-world scenarios.

name: AlgoBid

meta.organizer: ZEAL COLLEGE OF ENGINEERING & RESEARCH, PUNE
meta.tagline: Bid Smart. Code Faster. Win Bigger.

description: This is a high-energy, strategy-driven competitive programming event that combines the thrill of live auctions with algorithmic problem-solving. Teams use a starting budget of $1000 Virtual Money (VM) to bid in real-time to acquire coding problems.
requirements: Intel Core i3 (8th Gen) / Ryzen 3 processor
VS Code (pre-installed) or any available IDE
Access to whitelisted judge platform (HackerRank / Codeforces / LeetCode Custom Judge)
rules: Every team MUST bid on every problem.
Minimum bid increment is $50 VM per raise.
Teams are limited to bidding only their current available Virtual Money.
Highest bidder wins the problem, and in case of a tie, the team that placed the bid fastest wins.
Each team can win at most ONE problem per round, and all bids are final.
Only the winning team may attempt to solve the problem.
Submissions must be made through the designated platform (e.g., HackerRank / LeetCode custom judge).
Scoring is binary (full points for correct, zero for incorrect), and no partial credit is awarded.
All code must be written during the event window.
Teams may request up to 2 hints per problem.
Each hint deducts 200 points from the final score.
Requests must be made in writing to the invigilator.
Round 2 may involve adjusted problem difficulty and point values, or use unsolved problems from Round 1.
The bidding time is reduced in Round 2 to increase pressure.
Use of the internet outside the designated judge platform is prohibited.
Inter-team communication, collaboration, or code similarity is prohibited.
Submitting pre-written or plagiarized solutions is prohibited.
Attempting to view another team's problem set or engaging in disruptive behavior is prohibited.
procedure: The event consists of 2 rounds.
Each round follows an Auction + Solve format.
Each round has a total duration of 90 minutes.
Auction Phase lasts 20 minutes.
Solving Phase lasts 70 minutes.
Teams bid on problems using Virtual Money.
Winning teams solve the acquired problems to earn points.
judging_criteria: Hint deductions (200 points per hint, maximum 2 per problem)
Final Score = Sum of points from solved problems - (200 x hints used)
outcome: The event tests both algorithmic thinking and economic decision-making. It creates pressure and rewards teams that are strategic and disciplined, rather than just the best coders. The event rewards clarity of thought over chaos, and the winner is the best thinker.

Harassment, discrimination, or inappropriate behavior will not be tolerated.

procedure: Teams are formed and briefed.

Teams brainstorm and finalize their idea.
They build a basic model (idea, features, business plan).
Prepare a pitch deck.
The final presentation is given to judges.

where participants use their own idea or a provided problem statement.

round, where a single common theme is revealed at the start, focusing on adaptability and depth of implementation.

requirements: Participants must use only MATLAB software for the competition.
The use of external storage devices, such as USBs, is prohibited.
Participants are not allowed to use pre-written code.
rules: Internet access is restricted unless specifically permitted or specified for the task.

Major Round is 60 minutes long, where candidates must solve 10 problem statements.

additional_details.circuit_simulation_note: For Circuit Simulation, Round 1 is a 30-minute MCQ session covering theory and circuit identification. The second round is a 60-minute session focused on building circuits using PROTEUS.

name: Blind Coding

meta.organizer: ZEAL COLLEGE OF ENGINEERING & RESEARCH, PUNE

description: A unique programming challenge where participants must write code without executing it or viewing any output during development. It tests syntax accuracy, logic clarity, and coding confidence.
requirements: Participants must use the provided IDE/editor.
Allowed Languages: C, C++, Java, or Python.
rules: No compilation or execution is allowed during the coding phase.
Internet access, mobile phones, and external help are strictly prohibited.
Code will be compiled and executed by judges after time ends.
Participants will be disqualified for running code before the allowed time.
Participants will be disqualified for plagiarism, using unfair means, or misbehavior.
procedure: Participants are given 1-2 programming problems.
They must write complete code without executing it.
After submission, judges compile and run the code.
Evaluation is based on execution results and correctness.

additional_details.objective: To improve coding accuracy, syntax knowledge, and logical thinking while encouraging the writing of error-free code on the first attempt without trial-and-error.
additional_details.eligibility: The competition is open to all students (Diploma, UG, or PG).
additional_details.registration_note: Pre-registration is mandatory. Seats are limited based on lab capacity, and each participant is assigned a unique ID.
additional_details.scoring_system: Full marks for correct solution. Partial marking for logical correctness despite minor errors. Zero marks for compilation errors.
additional_details.result_declaration: Results are determined by maximum test cases passed and least errors. In case of a tie, time taken is the deciding factor.
additional_details.organizer_rights: Organizers reserve the right to modify rules, change difficulty levels, and disqualify participants for misconduct.

name: Circle Cricket

meta.organizer: ZEAL COLLEGE OF ENGINEERING & RESEARCH, PUNE

description: Circle Cricket is a fast-paced, entertaining variation of traditional cricket where players stand in a circular formation, creating a dynamic and unpredictable game.
rules: Participants must maintain fair play and sportsmanship.
Arguments with organizers or the umpire are not allowed, as the umpire's decision is final.
Teams or players can be disqualified for misconduct, abusive behavior, cheating, rule violations, or dangerous play.
Organizers hold the final authority in disputes and reserve the right to modify rules based on ground conditions.
procedure: The fielding team stands in a circle with one batter positioned at the center.
A bowler is selected from the fielding team.
Each team is allotted one innings consisting of 04 overs.
Roles are rotated after the innings.
judging_criteria: The team that has accumulated the highest number of runs at the end of the match is declared the winner.

additional_details.objective: The batting team aims to score maximum runs by hitting the ball into designated score circles, while the fielding team tries to restrict runs and get the batter out quickly.
additional_details.ground_setup: The fielding team stands in a circle with one batter positioned at the center. A bowler is then selected from the fielding team.

gameplay_rules.batting: The batter hits the ball in any direction within the circle. Runs are determined by where the ball's second bump occurs. A batter is out if they miss the ball, if the ball is caught after one bump without entering a score circle, or if the ball's first bump is beyond the third circle.
gameplay_rules.bowling: Only underarm bowling is permitted. Fast or dangerous bowling is prohibited and results in 2 bonus runs for the batter. Each bowler is limited to one over.
gameplay_rules.fielding: One fielder is placed after each of the three circles. Fielders may not enter restricted circles; doing so awards the batter 1 bonus run.

additional_details.registration_note: Pre-registration is mandatory with a fee of 200/- per team. Teams are accepted on a first-come, first-served basis and must use a unique ID inspired by the

name: AlignX

description: Line Follower Challenge involving autonomous robots navigating complex tracks.
rules: Robots must be completely autonomous and wireless (no Bluetooth, RF, or WiFi).
Maximum weight of 3kg (5% tolerance).
Robot must fit within 20cm x 20cm x 20cm.
Maximum voltage must not exceed 16.8V DC.
Must include fail-safe mechanism.
Must support at least two autonomous speed modes (
and
Shortcuts or skipping sections are strictly prohibited.
No flammable, hazardous, or high-voltage materials allowed.
All components must be securely assembled.
procedure: Round 1: Preliminary (Timed runs on standard track) – 3 hours
Round 2: Finals (Top 10 teams compete) – 2 hours
Final Evaluation: Judging & Prize Distribution – 1 hour

additional_details.objective: Build a fully autonomous robot to follow a thick black line on a white surface with turns, curves, intersections, and crossovers.
additional_details.track_details: 3cm thick black line on white background with hairpin bends, acute angles, and possible color inversions.
additional_details.efficiency_bonus: Bonus points awarded for maximum distance per mAh consumed.

name: Contraptions

meta.organizer: ZEAL COLLEGE OF ENGINEERING & RESEARCH, PUNE

description: Energy Conversion – Hit & Drop Challenge involving chain-reaction based systems.
rules: System must include at least 3 energy conversions.
Each step must automatically trigger the next.
No manual interference between steps.
Maximum of 3 human interventions allowed.
Bottle must be hit using mechanism (no hand interaction).
Bottle must drop at least 1 foot (≈30 cm).
No sharing of materials.
Use of waste materials is encouraged.
Design must be safe and stable.
procedure: Initial trigger starts chain reaction.
Energy conversion steps execute sequentially.
Bottle is hit and displaced.
Final drop must occur at required distance.

additional_details.eligibility: Open to undergraduate students with valid ID
additional_details.example_flow: Potential energy → kinetic → mechanical → elastic → bottle hit → displacement

name: RoboRush

meta.organizer: ZEAL COLLEGE OF ENGINEERING & RESEARCH, PUNE

description: Robotics competition focused on obstacle navigation, path planning, and speed optimization.
rules: Only one robot per team.
Robot must not exceed 30cm x 30cm size.
Maximum weight: 1–1.5 kg.
Robot must not jump, fly, climb, or damage arena.
No marking arena or leaving parts behind.
No touching robot after track reveal.
No pre-fed maps allowed.
procedure: Round 1: Basic Level – simple track with 4 checkpoints, 3 runs, best run considered.
Round 2: Advanced Level – complex track with obstacles, 3 runs, up to 5 checkpoints.

additional_details.objective: Navigate from start to end with fastest and cleanest run.
